title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its first launch on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA and Draper have ended a lunar lander mission due to major delays in spacecraft development. The termination follows prolonged setbacks in the project's timeline.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made it a significant player in capital markets, highlighting its vision of interplanetary civilization. The company's main challenge now is securing more launch and operational space.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the U.S.-led initiative for lunar exploration and cooperation. This move comes over two years after Serbia joined China’s lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 due to some of the vehicle's engines failing to ignite. This marks another setback in the development of the Starship program.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for York Space Systems, contributing to the Space Development Agency's Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partially funded by unspecified sources.

title: Defense Innovation Unit seeks commercial path to deliver electrical power from orbit
summary: The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is seeking a commercial approach to deliver electrical power from orbit by fiscal 2030, aiming to advance long-studied space-based solar power technology for military use.

title: Building the infrastructure for a shifting space economy with Massi Ladovaz
summary: SpinLaunch CEO Massi Ladovaz discusses reimagining spacecraft launch methods and the challenges of building satellite constellations in a shifting space economy. The conversation highlights the evolving infrastructure needs of the space industry.

title: Trump’s pick to lead Space Force defends plan to more than double budget
summary: Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess, Trump's nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force, defended plans to more than double the Space Force's budget during a Senate Armed Services Committee confirmation hearing on July 16.

title: 'We knew we had a lot to live up to': As 'Aliens' turns 40, we chat to the legendary VFX masters who created the Alien Queen (interview)
summary: An interview with VFX masters Shane Mahan and Lindsay MacGowan from Legacy Effects, who worked on the 1986 film 'Aliens' and created the Alien Queen. The article reflects on the legacy of the film as it turns 40.

title: Beyond the Perseids: 6 meteor showers worth watching this summer
summary: Six lesser-known meteor showers will be visible this summer, offering alternatives to the popular Perseids. These events provide opportunities for stargazers to observe celestial phenomena.

title: Remembering 'Ulysses 31', the '80s cartoon that transported 'The Odyssey' to outer space
summary: The news reflects on 'Ulysses 31', an 80s cartoon that reimagined 'The Odyssey' in outer space, highlighting its iconic theme song and cultural impact.

title: These 'metallic' dunes on Mars look like sci-fi. What are they really?
summary: New images from ESA's Mars Express orbiter reveal unusual metallic-looking dunes on Mars, resembling science fiction. These formations are likely shaped by unique geological processes on the Red Planet.

title: 'Aliens' at 40: Here are 26 reasons why James Cameron's sequel is still the greatest sci-fi action movie of all time
summary: The article celebrates the 40th anniversary of James Cameron's 'Aliens' as the greatest sci-fi action movie of all time, outshining other films in the genre like 'Predator'.

title: Earth's largest particle accelerator opens new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang: 'A culmination of a decades-long quest'
summary: Earth's largest particle accelerator has opened a new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang, marking a decades-long quest. This observation promises new insights into the evolution of the early universe.

title: 'The dawn of a new space era': Vikram-1, India's 1st private orbital rocket, aces debut launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully completed its debut orbital launch on July 18, marking India's first private orbital rocket mission and making India the third nation with private orbital launch capability.

title: 'The Starry Night' in space: Dark Energy Camera channels a cosmic Van Gogh (video)
summary: The Dark Energy Camera captured a vibrant image of the Corona Australis Molecular Cloud, resembling Van Gogh's 'The Starry Night'. This cosmic view highlights star-forming regions in space.

title: Smoke from devastating Canada wildfires visible from space | Space photo of the day for July 17, 2026
summary: Satellites captured images of smoke from devastating Canada wildfires, making the smoke visible from space. This event highlights the scale of the wildfires and their impact on air quality.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, truss-braced wing design (SWEET-15) and found encouraging results even when pushed beyond its intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing demonstrated promising structural performance.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image showing the crescent of Mars growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in 2026. The sequence illustrates Mars' appearance from farthest to closest during the approach.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) formed a working group to review updated VTE case information and discuss progress on mitigating venous thromboembolism (VTE) risks during spaceflight. The group analyzed altered blood flow data from astronauts to develop a VTE Risk Score algorithm.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC a contract to provide operations, maintenance, and repair services for Ames Research Center. The five-year hybrid contract ensures the center's facilities remain operational to support NASA's missions and partners.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE observatories have released a composite image studying a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula. The image combines X-ray data from both observatories to analyze the neutron star's properties.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than August 30, 2024. The telescope's launch is planned for 7:20 a.m. EDT on that date.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on Sunday, Aug. 30, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026, using its multispectral imager instrument. The spacecraft traveled from northeast to southwest over Mars during the six-minute imaging process.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson participated in a virtual signing ceremony in the island nation.

title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
summary: The Bijagós archipelago in Guinea-Bissau is rich in biodiversity, supporting migratory shorebirds and sea turtles due to its diverse ecosystems. The region's mudflats, beaches, and mangrove forests contribute to this ecological abundance.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition, preventing a scheduled launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This mission marks another crewed flight to the ISS, continuing the ongoing operations aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. The telescope is undergoing final preparations before its upcoming mission.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines. This milestone brings the company closer to its next major launch.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the company delayed the development of its Neutron rocket. The gains in Electron's operations contrasted with setbacks in Neutron's timeline.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights from SpaceX are also expected.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to be the world's first commercial space station.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, highlighting planned activities and industrial opportunities. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications, aiming to enhance robotic operations in orbit. This innovation could significantly improve satellite servicing and maintenance tasks.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The mission is a success of the programme, which facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured $10 million in a seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has selected SpaceX's Starlink to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission. This contract highlights Starlink's expanding role in space exploration beyond Earth orbit.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the organization's future following its liquidation. The interview explores the vision and next steps for ISU beyond this transition.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has secured $70 million in Series C funding to expand its operations and meet the growing demand for sovereign space hardware. The investment will support the company's scaling efforts in the space industry.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's (OSC) proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its financial viability. The event highlighted concerns over the OSC's ability to manage the new framework.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The article discusses concerns about a potential sales freeze on SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which could disrupt the space industry's reliance on its reliable launch services. This uncertainty raises existential questions for numerous satellite businesses that depend on SpaceX's launch capabilities.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital purchased new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The acquisition aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's space acquisition nominee testified that legacy military space programs were not designed for modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of companies in the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access in Australia. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure for space missions.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on lunar exploration, focusing on technical information exchange and system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, using a machine-to-machine pipeline developed with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will use SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness. This is the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and disrupting Chinese military space capabilities as key deterrence measures. His nomination highlights the focus on enhancing U.S. space defense against adversarial threats.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the race to establish space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies will lead an ESA program to explore blockchain frameworks for enhancing 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) security. The collaboration involves detecting anomalies in satellite-based 5G networks.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat showcased a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This demonstration highlights advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also initiated a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth.
